creator: bapt@FreeBSD.org
text:
On Tue, Mar 11, 2014 at 07:59:06PM -0500, Bryan Drewery wrote:
> Thank you for working on this. However I wonder why make this change?
> 
> This goes against our plans to have all ports depend only on ports. I
> admit this has not been communicated well. libexecinfo should probably
> be moved to /usr/lib/private on head to prevent ports from using it.
> 
> On a side note, this change would be better as a Mk/Uses/execinfo.mk and
> having a USES=execinfo to not have all the duplicated logic. Similar to
> readline.mk. However, we are actively working to move libreadline into
> /usr/lib/private as well and then ensure ports are using the port version.
> 
> Also, many of these ports (before the change) appear to already support
> the base version. Since it is LIB_DEPENDS= libexecinfo.so, if the
> library is not found in the ld path, it will pull in devel/libexecinfo.
> On 10+ it will find it in the path and NOT pull in devel/libexecinfo. So
> the check for OSVERSION is not needed. The port version is only forced
> with LIB_DEPENDS= ${LOCALBASE}/lib/libexecinfo.so and setting LDFLAGS to
> -L ${LOCALBASE}/lib.
> 
> -- 
> Regards,
> Bryan Drewery
> 

I disagree in that case, libexecinfo has a stable API/ABI and is not intended to
be broken any soon, it is compatible both abi and api with the one in ports
except that the one in ports is a quick and dirty code.

I see for that particular case no gain at pushing it into private at all

Concerning the PR there is no gain with it LIB_DEPENDS will first anyway catch
up libexecinfo from base and use it and thus not pull devel/libexecinfo at all
(or I am missing something) because the libexecinfo.so file dependency will be
satisfied by base.

regards,
Bapt
